topic.name: Veterans Affairs Funding and Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to the funding and improvement of services and programs for veterans, particularly focusing on healthcare and support for homeless veterans.

Document 1: Increases and extends through FY2010 the annual authorization of appropriations for Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) comprehensive service programs for homeless veterans.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to waive the imposition or collection of copayments for telehealth and telemedicine visits of veterans.

Directs the Secretary to establish a five-year program of outreach to veterans with respect to access and use of federal, state, and local programs providing compensation and other benefits for service in the Armed Forces.

topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code affecting various aspects of taxation and income treatment.

taxed at individual income tax rates.

held by a taxpayer for more than three years for purposes of determining gain or loss.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income gain from the sale or exchange of a residential property acquired at foreclosure.
after default on a mortgage.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a corporation incorporated under the laws of Puerto Rico to elect to be treated as a domestic corporation.
deriving at least 50% of its gross income from sources within Puerto Rico.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow bona fide residents of the District of Columbia an exclusion from gross income for income derived from sources within the District of Columbia.
for income effectively connected with a trade or business within the District of Columbia.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income gain from the sale of qualified investment property acquired within 18 months.
Defines 'qualified investment property' as original issue stock in a community bank.

topic.name: FDA Regulatory Acts
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation and oversight of drugs and medical devices by the FDA.

Document 1: Directs the Commissioner of Food and Drugs (FDA) to complete a study to examine the classification of ultraviolet tanning lamps
Requires the Commissioner to carry out recommendations made in a report on labeling requirements for indoor tanning devices.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow biosimilars to be marketed seven years after the reference brand name biological product is licensed.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish a criminal fine and/or imprisonment for a person who knowingly causes a prescription drug to be adulterated
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to educate the public and health care professionals on counterfeit drugs.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to apply debarment provisions currently applicable to abbreviated new drugs to any drug products
Requires the Secretary to annually report to Congress on the implementation of debarment provisions.
Document 5: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow a person to submit an application for licensure of a biological product based on its similarity to a licensed biological product
Sets forth requirements for a determination by the Secretary that a biological product is interchangeable with a reference product.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to authorize importation of life-saving drugs for which there is, or is likely to be, a shortage.
The FDA may deny importation of a drug for reasons related to safety or effectiveness.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to revise provisions governing the importation of prescription drugs.
Requires the Secretary to educate consumers regarding prescription drug importation.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Human Rights
topic.description: This cluster discusses U.S. legislative measures aimed at promoting human rights, religious freedom, and democratic governance in various regions, particularly in the Near East, South Central Asia, and Southeast Asia.

Document 1: Directs the President to appoint a Special Envoy to Promote Religious Freedom of Religious Minorities in the Near East and South Central Asia
monitor and combat acts of religious intolerance and incitement targeted against such religious minorities.
Document 2: Prohibits U.S. nonhumanitarian assistance to the government of Vietnam unless the federal government provides assistance supporting human rights training
the government of Vietnam has made substantial progress respecting political, media, and religious freedoms.
Document 3: Prohibits FY2013 funds for any U.S. department or agency from being used to provide assistance to the Republic of Georgia until the Secretary of State reports to Congress that the parliamentary elections were carried out in a free and competitive manner
the presence and findings of election observers.

topic.name: Environmental Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to environmental protection, particularly concerning water quality and invasive species management.

Document 1: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to direct the EPA to publish a health advisory for perchlorate.
Require consumer confidence reports by community water systems to disclose the presence of perchlorate.
Document 3: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to require the EPA to promulgate a national primary drinking water regulation for perchlorate.
Document 4: Amends the Solid Waste Disposal Act to allow states to prohibit the importation of solid waste from outside the state.
Directs the EPA to identify alternative solid waste disposal methods.

topic.name: Sanctions and Nonproliferation Policies
topic.description: The documents focus on U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions and preventing the proliferation of weapons and nuclear capabilities in Iran, Syria, and North Korea.

Document 1: Amends the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996 to expand the definitions of 'petroleum resources' and 'person.'
Subjects Iran to specified economic sanctions unless the President certifies to Congress that Iran has verifiably dismantled all nuclear enrichment-related and reprocessing-related programs.
Document 2: Directs the President to impose three or more sanctions on a person that knowingly makes an investment of at least $20 million that contributes to Syria's ability to develop petroleum resources.
Authorizes the President to impose three or more sanctions on a person that knowingly sells or provides to Syria refined petroleum products.
Document 3: Directs the President to impose sanctions for not less than two years on any foreign person who...transferred to or acquired from Iran, Syria, or North Korea...certain listed nuclear, dual use, missile, chemical, biological, toxic, or nonlisted but otherwise prohibited goods, services, or technology.
Prohibits U.S. nuclear cooperation agreements and related export licenses and transfers of...
Document 4: Directs the President to impose visa entry and financial sanctions on a person determined to be complicit in human rights abuses committed against Iranian citizens.
Terminates sanctions upon presidential certification to Congress that the government of Iran has...ceased its practices of violence and abuse of Iranian citizens.
Document 6: Expresses the sense of Congress that China cannot be allowed to interfere with the free use of waters and airspace in the South China Sea and the East China Sea.

The bill requires the President to impose entry and U.S.-based property sanctions with respect to...any Chinese person that contributes to construction or development projects in areas of the South China Sea contested by one or more members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations.

topic.name: Educator Development and Equity
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving educator effectiveness, supporting teacher training, and ensuring equitable access to quality education for disadvantaged students.

Document 1: Revises, and reauthorizes appropriations under, part A of title II of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.
Requires each state applying for an allotment of part A funds to develop a state educator equity plan to improve access of low-income and minority students to effective educators.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to local educational agencies (LEAs) or charter schools to provide high school students with relevant academic and career-related learning opportunities.

Requires grant applicants to make specified equity assurances, including that their LEAs will allocate school improvement funds to eligible high schools in amounts that are proportionate to those schools' share of low-income students.
Document 5: Requires the Department of Education to award competitive matching grants to partnerships between high-need local educational agencies (LEAs) and institutions of higher education to establish or support teacher preparation programs.
Grants may be used for activities designed to improve the quality of education in high-need areas.
Document 6: Revises and establishes programs relating to recruitment, preparation, distribution, and retention of public elementary and secondary school teachers and principals.
Requires states to provide assurances of their reasonable progress toward equitable access to teacher quality as a condition of assistance under ESEA.

topic.name: Health Insurance Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at regulating health insurance practices, ensuring consumer protection, and enhancing the transparency and accountability of insurance providers.

Document 1: federal provisions requiring the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to review unreasonable premium increases
corrective actions to ensure that any excessive, unjustified, or unfairly discriminatory rates are corrected
Document 2: nothing in that Act shall modify, impair, or supersede the operation of any of the antitrust laws with respect to the business of health insurance
prohibitions against using unfair methods of competition to the business of health insurance
Document 4: exclude remuneration paid for licensed independent insurance producers from administrative cost calculations
defines 'independent insurance producer' to mean an insurance agent or broker

defines 'independent insurance producer' to mean an insurance agent or broker

prohibits the issuance or renewal of a Medigap policy in any state unless the policy can be expected to return to policyholders
Document 7: directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to request the National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) to conduct reviews

topic.name: Energy Policy and Taxation
topic.description: The documents collectively address legislative measures related to energy production, alternative fuels, and taxation policies affecting the energy sector.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exempt sales of natural gas for use in motor vehicles from retail sales limitation.
Focus on natural gas and its role in energy security.
Document 2: Prohibits the use of funds for non-alternative fuel vehicles by Congress members.
Emphasizes the promotion of alternative fuel resources.
Document 3: Imposes a windfall profit tax on crude oil and natural gas.
Dedicates proceeds to low-income home energy assistance.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make fuels derived from animal fats ineligible for tax credits.
Targets tax incentives for biodiesel and renewable diesel.
Document 5: Amends Clean Air Act provisions governing the Renewable Fuel Program.
Requires reductions in renewable fuel volumes based on available biofuel.
Document 6: Revises tax deduction requirements for vehicle contributions.
Addresses charitable contributions related to motor vehicles.
Document 7: Makes permanent income and excise tax credits for alcohol and biodiesel.
Focuses on tax incentives to promote renewable fuels.

topic.name: Social Security Reform
topic.description: Legislative amendments aimed at modifying the Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance (OASDI) provisions of the Social Security Act.

Document 1: Amends title II of the Social Security Act with respect to the determination of a valid marriage
accounts for marriages in jurisdictions other than a state
Document 2: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to repeal the government pension offset requirement
reducing insurance benefits with respect to federal, state, or local government employees
Document 3: Eliminate the two-year waiting period following a divorce for a divorced spouse to become entitled to a spouse's OASDI benefits
Document 4: Directs the Bureau of Labor Statistics to prepare and publish a Consumer Price Index for Elderly Consumers
Amends title II of the Social Security Act to tie the CPIEC to computation of cost-of-living increases in OASDI benefits
Document 5: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to modify the formula for determining the amount of reduced monthly OASDI benefits
Declares that such benefit reductions shall be equal to the amount by which two-thirds of the total amount of the combined monthly benefit exceeds $1,200
Document 6: Establish in the Federal Old-Age and Survivors Insurance Trust Fund a Social Security Surplus Protection Account
Denies the availability of the balance in the Account for investment by the Managing Trustee
Document 7: Increases the contribution and benefit base for 2017 through 2019
Revises the computation of primary insurance amounts to include surplus average indexed monthly earnings

topic.name: Health Care and Privacy Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at preventing fraud in health care, protecting personal health information, and regulating the use of technology in health care and surveillance.

Document 1: Establishes in the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) the Office of the Deputy Secretary for Health Care Fraud Prevention.
Requires the Office to direct the implementation within HHS of health care fraud prevention and detection recommendations.
Document 2: Prohibits a federal entity from using unmanned aircraft or other mobile aerial-view devices (MAVDs) to surveil property, persons, or their effects.
Exceptions allow MAVDs to be used by federal entities to surveil national borders or national waters to prevent or deter illegal entry of persons or illegal substances.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to study and identify any potential threats to privacy protections posed by the integration of unmanned aircraft (drone) systems.

Prohibits the Secretary from approving, issuing, or awarding any certificate, license, or other grant of authority to operate a drone system unless the application includes a data collection statement.
Document 4: Establish policies and procedures to streamline the process of obtaining state and national criminal history background checks on private security officers.
Establish a criminal history review program to provide covered entities with reliable and accurate information on the criminal history of a private security officer.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FDCA) to apply it to medical software to the same extent and in the same manner as it applies to devices.
Defines 'medical software' as software that is intended to be marketed to directly change the structure or any function of the body of man or other animals.
Document 6: Instruct the Director of Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to establish policies, procedures, and standards for agencies to follow in the event of a breach of data security.
Requires such policies and procedures to include timely notification to individuals whose sensitive personal information could be compromised.
Document 7: Sets forth rights of individuals to protect their personal health information, including the right to inspect and copy such information.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to develop and disseminate guidelines for the establishment of safeguards and procedures for use under this Act.

topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative reforms related to judicial proceedings, including coverage of court sessions, investigative hearings, and attorney compensation.

temporary district judges for various federal judicial districts.

obtain national and state criminal history background checks on their volunteers.
Document 5: require a process to allow private security guard employees to challenge their criminal history records
prohibit private security guard employers from hiring guards without obtaining certain state criminal history information.
Document 6: increase from $65 to $80 the hourly rate of attorneys representing indigent defendants
cap for representation of a defendant before the District of Columbia Court of Appeals.
Document 7: Authorizes the presiding judge to permit the photographing, electronic recording, broadcasting, or televising
violation of the due process rights of any party.

topic.name: Mental Health and Elder Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving mental health services and protections for vulnerable older adults.

Document 1: Excellence in Mental Health Act - Amends the Public Health Service Act to set forth criteria for the certification of federally-qualified community behavioral health centers.

Requires the Administrator of the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration to certify, and recertify at least every five years, federally qualified community behavioral health centers.
Document 2: Health Outcomes, Planning, and Education for Alzheimer's Act - Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to cover comprehensive Alzheimer's disease and related dementia diagnosis and services.
Document 3: Elder Protection and Abuse Prevention Act - Amends the Older Americans Act of 1965 to direct the Assistant Secretary of Health and Human Services for Aging to ensure that all programs funded under such Act include appropriate training in elder abuse prevention.
Makes it a duty and function of the Administration on Aging to establish priority information and assistance services for older individuals.
Document 4: Improving Care for Vulnerable Older Citizens through Workforce Advancement Act - Amends the Older Americans Act of 1965 to direct the Assistant Secretary of Aging to carry out a program awarding grants to eligible entities to carry out six separate demonstration projects that focus on care coordination and service delivery for older individuals.

Designing and testing new models of care coordination and service delivery that thoughtfully and effectively deploy advanced aides to improve efficiency and quality of care for frail older individuals.
Document 5: Responding to Urgent Needs of Survivors of the Holocaust Act - Amends the Older Americans Act of 1965 to include specifically within its purview older Americans who are Holocaust survivors.

Require a state nutrition project to encourage individuals who distribute nutrition services to engage in conversation with homebound older individuals and to be aware of the warning signs of medical emergencies, injury, or abuse.
Document 6: Embedded Mental Health Providers for Reserves Act of 2011 - Directs the Secretary of the military department concerned to provide to each reserve member performing inactive-duty training access to mental health assessments with a licensed mental health professional.
Requires each reserve member participating in annual training or individual duty training to have access, during such participation, to specified behavioral health support programs.
Document 7: Occupational Therapy in Mental Health Act - Amends the Public Health Service Act to include occupational therapists within the definition of 'behavioral and mental health professionals' for purposes of the National Health Service Corps.

topic.name: Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Reform
topic.description: Legislative amendments aimed at modifying the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA) and related laws to address surveillance practices and privacy concerns.

Document 1: Amends the USA PATRIOT Improvement and Reauthorization Act of 2005 to extend ... roving electronic surveillance authority.
Amends the FISA Amendments Act of 2008 to extend ... electronic surveillance of certain persons outside the United States.
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to impose limits on roving electronic surveillance.
Directs the AG to establish minimization and destruction procedures.
Document 4: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA), with respect to provisions governing the use and disclosure of information obtained under such Act.
Requires the application of rules for the use in court proceedings of classified information under the Classified Information Procedures Act.
Document 6: This bill amends the federal criminal code to expand the categories of subscriber transactional records that the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) may request.
The bill also amends the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004 to make permanent the government's authority to track a non-U.S. person who engages in international terrorism.

topic.name: Early Childhood Education and Literacy
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative initiatives aimed at improving access to high-quality early childhood education and promoting literacy among young children.

Document 1: Pediatric Early Literacy Programs
health care providers encourage parents to read aloud
Document 2: increase the rates and maximum allowable amount of the tax credit for employer-provided child care
tax credit for child care providers who hold a bachelor's degree in early childhood education
Document 3: Pediatric Early Literacy program
health care providers give each visiting child a new children's book
Document 4: expand access to high-quality child care for infants and toddlers
increase parental options for and access to such care

maximum class size of 20 children

require each teacher to have at least an associate degree in early childhood education
Document 7: high-quality full day voluntary prekindergarten programs
ensure that each classroom is taught by a teacher who has at least a baccalaureate degree in early childhood education

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Environmental Protection
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to the management, protection, and remediation of federal lands and resources in the United States.

Document 1: Encourages the partial or complete remediation of inactive and abandoned mine sites for the public good
Defines 'inactive or abandoned mine site' as a mine site located in the United States that is no longer actively mined
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to acquire certain parcels of land in exchange for certain parcels of National Forest System lands
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture to require a permit for commercial filming activities on federal land
Prohibits the Secretary from assessing any fees in addition to the annual fee described above
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to transfer identified public land to the administrative jurisdiction of the Secretary of the Navy
Withdraws the public land to be transferred by this Act from all forms of appropriation under the public land laws
Document 5: Prohibits the Secretaries of the Interior or Agriculture from taking any action that would permanently close any aircraft landing strip located on land under their jurisdiction
Requires the Secretaries to adopt a nationwide policy for governing backcountry aviation issues
Document 6: Designates certain land as the 'Sabinoso Wilderness' and as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System
Withdraws the lands identified as 'Land Withdrawn From Mineral Entry' from all forms of entry, appropriation, and disposal under the public land laws
Document 7: Withdraws specified National Forest System land from all forms of appropriation or disposal under the public land laws
Prohibits any prohibition on activities outside the boundaries of the withdrawal area that can be seen or heard from within the boundaries

topic.name: Legislative Measures on Public Safety and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing public safety, regulatory oversight, and accountability in different sectors including health, education, and finance.

Document 1: requires the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), in coordination with the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), to carry out a pilot program to provide eligible anthrax vaccines
DHS must select between two and five states for voluntary participation in the program
Document 2: establish a voluntary, alternative accreditation system for higher education providers
Providers authorized using this process are eligible for Pell Grant and other federal funding
Document 3: bars an American Indian Group seeking federal tribal acknowledgment that does not submit a letter of intent
terminates the Federal Recognition and Acknowledgment Process by the earlier of three years after enactment of this Act
Document 4: conditions any advance payment of premium tax credits and cost-sharing reductions
must satisfy specified requirements with respect to automation, effectiveness, and cooperation
Document 5: require the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to establish a third-party quality system assessment program
an accredited person must submit a summary of their assessment
Document 6: states that it shall be Department of Defense (DOD) policy that flyovers of public events may only be flown as part of an approved training mission
DOD shall maintain records of flyover requests and approvals
Document 7: directs the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to issue regulations to include as a qualified mortgage any mortgage loan
The Bureau shall assess these regulations, addressing their usage by community financial institutions

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures concerning abortion, including informed consent requirements, funding restrictions, and the protection of women's health rights.

Document 1: Prohibits federal funds or other assistance from being made available to any state or political subdivision to establish or implement any requirement that individuals receive vaccination for human papillomavirus (HPV).
Document 2: Requires abortion providers, before a woman gives informed consent to any part of an abortion, to perform an obstetric ultrasound on the pregnant woman.
Document 3: Requires abortion providers to perform an obstetric ultrasound on the pregnant woman, display the ultrasound images, and provide a medical description of the ultrasound images of the unborn child's cardiac activity.
Document 4: Prohibits any government from imposing on abortion services a requirement that a medical professional perform specific tests or medical procedures.
Document 6: Prohibits the expenditure of funds authorized or appropriated by federal law for any abortion.
Document 7: Prohibit federal family planning funds from being awarded to any grantees who perform abortions or whose subgrantees perform abortions.

topic.name: Labor Relations and Employee Rights
topic.description: This cluster focuses on amendments to labor laws aimed at enhancing employee representation, rights, and the processes governing collective bargaining.

Document 1: Amends the Railway Labor Act to require the National Mediation Board to apply the same procedures, including voting standards, to the direct decertification of a labor organization as are applied to elections to certify a representative.
Document 2: Employee Rights Act - Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation to collectively bargain.
Adds a requirement that representatives be selected by secret ballot in an election conducted by the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) by a majority of the employees in a unit.
Document 3: National Right-to-Work Act - Amends the National Labor Relations Act and the Railway Labor Act to repeal those provisions that permit employers, pursuant to a collective bargaining agreement that is a union security agreement, to require employees to join a union as a condition of employment.
Document 4: Re-empowerment of Skilled and Professional Employees and Construction Tradesworkers Act or the RESPECT Act - Amends the National Labor Relations Act to revise the definition of 'supervisor' in the following ways.
Document 5: Representation Fairness Restoration Act - Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to revise requirements for determination by the National Labor Relation Board (NLRB) of an appropriate bargaining unit before an election of collective bargaining representation.
Document 6: Employee Privacy Protection Act - Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board to acquire from the employer a list of all employees eligible to vote in the election.

topic.name: Victim Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at enhancing protections and support for victims of crime, particularly in cases of domestic violence, child exploitation, and sexual assault.

Document 1: Directs the Attorney General to make grants to i-SAFE, Inc., and to establish and administer competitive grant programs for organizations, to carry out Internet crime prevention education programs.

Defines 'Internet crime prevention education program' to mean a program for educating parents, children, educators, and communities to recognize and prevent potentially criminal activity on the Internet.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to expand the jurisdictional basis for prosecutions of sexual exploitation of children, selling or buying of children, or child pornography crimes.
Defines 'possess' with respect to crimes of child sexual exploitation and child pornography to include accessing by computer visual depictions of child pornography with the intent to view.
Document 3: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require each institution of higher education to include in its annual security report a statement of policy regarding its sexual assault and other intimate partner violence programs.
Requires such procedures to include the provision, in writing, to students or employees that report to the IHE that they have been the victim of such an offense...
Document 4: Prohibits threats or acts of violence against a person's pet under the offenses of stalking and interstate violation of a protection order.
Expresses the sense of Congress that states should encourage the inclusion of protections against violent or threatening acts against the pet of the person in domestic violence protection orders.
Document 5: Includes among the eligibility criteria for certain federal grants state laws authorizing gun confiscation by law enforcement officers in certain domestic violence cases.
Authorizes grants for training programs to establish integrated domestic violence courts.
Document 6: Requires a state that is receiving funds for certain law enforcement assistance programs to adopt laws and policies that prohibit parole for individuals convicted of a criminal sexual offense against an elderly victim.
Requests the U.S. Sentencing Commission to promptly review its guidelines for sexual offenses committed against the elderly.

topic.name: Federal Criminal Law Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to federal laws concerning penalties and enforcement mechanisms related to immigration, public safety, and national security.

Document 1: Migratory Bird Treaty Act Penalty and Enforcement Act of 2009
amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to subject a person who kills or wounds any migratory bird in an aggravated manner
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to provide that an alien who fails to appear at a removal proceeding shall be fined and imprisoned
Document 3: Prohibits the use of community oriented policing services funds in contravention of provisions of the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act
Document 4: Amends the federal criminal code to provide that existing prohibitions against conduct that endangers the safe navigation of a ship
Document 5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include engaging in or purposefully and materially supporting hostilities against the United States
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of State to offer a reward of up to $5 million to any individual who furnishes information leading to the arrest or conviction
Document 7: This bill amends the federal criminal code to declare that any person who knowingly engages in any scheme or artifice to defraud an individual of veterans' benefits

topic.name: Water Reclamation and Rights
topic.description: Legislation related to the planning, construction, and management of water reclamation projects and water rights for various communities and tribes.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of projects to treat impaired surface water, reclaim and reuse impaired groundwater
Limits the federal share of each of the project costs to 25%.
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water projects
Limits the federal share of each project's cost to 25%.
Document 3: provide for the issuance of tax-exempt clean renewable water supply bonds to finance certain water recycling, desalination, and groundwater remediation projects
establishes a national clean renewable water supply bond limitation.
Document 4: authorizes the Department of the Interior to carry out the projects entitled: the 'Dry-Redwater Regional Water Authority System'
defines the service areas of such projects in North Dakota and Montana.
Document 5: directs the Secretary of the Interior to allocate to the Blackfeet Tribe of the Blackfeet Indian Reservation of Montana the St. Mary River water right
holds the tribal water right in trust by the United States for the benefit of the Tribe.
Document 6: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of a project to reclaim and reuse wastewater in Sacramento County
provide recycled water to irrigate approximately 4,500 acres of land.
Document 7: authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the White Mountain Apache Tribe Water Rights Quantification Agreement
requires the water rights of the Tribe to be held in trust by the United States.

topic.name: Food and Drug Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at ensuring the safety and compliance of food, dietary supplements, and human tissue products in the United States.

Document 1: Requires the FDA to inspect establishments that engage in the manufacture of human cells, tissues, and cellular and tissue-based products.
Sets forth penalties for failing to comply with model form requirements or for knowingly using fraudulent information.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act to include in the definition of 'adulterated' any carcass or part thereof, meat, or meat food product that is contaminated with enterohemorrhagic (EHEC) Shiga toxin-producing serotypes of Escherichia coli.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to revise the nutritional information that restaurants and retail food establishments must disclose.
Defines 'reasonable basis' for a restaurant or similar food establishment’s nutrient content disclosures.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to expand the registration requirements for a dietary supplement manufacturing facility.
Deems a dietary supplement that does not meet the requirements of this Act to be misbranded.
Document 5: Prohibits the Department of Agriculture from awarding grants to support the marketing of alcohol products.
Document 6: Requires the Department of Agriculture to establish a national voluntary bioengineered food labeling standard.
USDA regulations must prohibit claims regarding the safety or quality of food based on whether or not the food is bioengineered.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish standards for good manufacturing practices for the minimal processing of produce.
Directs the Secretary to establish general standards for good agricultural practices for the production of raw agricultural commodities.

topic.name: Waterway Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts aimed at the conservation, restoration, and management of water bodies and related ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to reauthorize the National Estuary Program.
Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to evaluate the implementation of each comprehensive conservation and management plan.
Document 2: Establishes a nutria eradication program to restore nutria-damaged wetland.
Requires management, research, and public education activities.
Document 3: Directs the Forest Service to establish a Water Source Protection Program for National Forest System land.
Authorizes USDA to produce a water source management plan.
Document 4: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to extend grant funds for coral reef conservation projects.
NOAA is authorized to undertake emergency actions to stabilize, repair, or restore a coral reef.
Document 5: Amends the Harmful Algal Bloom and Hypoxia Research and Control Act to establish a National Harmful Algal Bloom and Hypoxia Program.
Directs the Under Secretary to coordinate efforts to develop a national strategy for harmful algal bloom events.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to establish financial reporting requirements for restoration activities in the Chesapeake Bay watershed.
Authorizes appropriations for FY2013-FY2018.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to require the establishment of a Columbia Basin Restoration Program.
Requires the Administrator to establish a Columbia River Basin Toxics Reduction Working Group.

topic.name: Tax Incentives for Social Programs
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at providing tax incentives and credits to support education, child care, veteran entrepreneurship, and family-related health expenses.

Document 1: establish in the Treasury the United States Library Trust Fund
allow taxpayers to designate a portion of any tax overpayment to the Fund
Document 2: allow business franchisors and veterans who purchase a franchise tax credits
provide information about the tax credits in this Act to veterans service organizations
Document 3: allow a refundable tax credit for the qualified education expenses
Defines 'qualified education expenses' as amounts paid for tuition and fees
Document 4: increase from $5,000 to $10,000 the limitation on the tax exclusion for employer-provided dependent care assistance
Provides for an annual inflation adjustment
Document 5: allow an income-based tax credit for 50% of qualified infertility treatment expenses
Defines 'qualified infertility treatment expenses' as amounts paid for the treatment of infertility
